    <h1 {...{
      "id": "displaying-inbox"
    }}>{`Displaying Inbox`}</h1>
    <p>{`This tutorial explains how to fetch and display an Inbox in your Android application.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "pre-requisites"
    }}>{`Pre-requisites`}</h2>
    <p><a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://developer.adobe.com/client-sdks/edge/adobe-journey-optimizer/#implement-extension-in-mobile-app"
      }}>{`Integrate and register AEPMessaging extension`}</a>{` in your app.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`The Inbox is a pre-built UI component that displays content cards in a unified container. Unlike individual content cards, the Inbox automatically manages loading states, error handling, empty states, and card layout based on server-side configuration from `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://business.adobe.com/products/journey-optimizer/adobe-journey-optimizer.html"
      }}>{`Adobe Journey Optimizer`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "fetch-inbox-settings-and-content-cards"
    }}>{`Fetch Inbox settings and Content Cards`}</h2>
    <p>{`To fetch the inbox settings and content cards for the surfaces configured in `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://business.adobe.com/products/journey-optimizer/adobe-journey-optimizer.html"
      }}>{`Adobe Journey Optimizer`}</a>{` campaigns, call the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://developer.adobe.com/client-sdks/edge/adobe-journey-optimizer/code-based/api-reference/#updatepropositionsforsurfaces"
      }}>{`updatePropositionsForSurfaces`}</a>{` API. You should batch requests for multiple surfaces in a single API call when possible. The returned inbox settings and content cards are cached in-memory by the Messaging extension and persist through the application's lifecycle.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`val surfaces = mutableListOf<Surface>()
surfaces.add(Surface("inbox"))
Messaging.updatePropositionsForSurfaces(surfaces)
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`This API call fetches the inbox and its content from the server and caches it locally. You should call this early in your app lifecycle (e.g., in your Application class or when the user logs in) to ensure content is available when the inbox is displayed.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "create-messaginginboxprovider"
    }}>{`Create MessagingInboxProvider`}</h2>
    <p>{`To display an Inbox, first create a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MessagingInboxProvider`}</inlineCode>{` with your configured surface. This provider is responsible for fetching inbox content and managing the inbox state through reactive updates.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin-1"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.messaging.MessagingInboxProvider
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.messaging.Surface

val surface = Surface("inbox")
val inboxProvider = MessagingInboxProvider(surface)
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MessagingInboxProvider`}</inlineCode>{` provides the following APIs:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`getInboxUI()`}</inlineCode>{` - Returns a `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://developer.android.com/kotlin/flow"
        }}>{`Flow`}</a>{` of `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`InboxUIState`}</inlineCode>{` representing the current state of the inbox.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`refresh()`}</inlineCode>{` - Manually refreshes the inbox content by fetching new propositions.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`The Inbox automatically handles layout (vertical/horizontal), styling, and unread indicators based on server-side configuration from Adobe Journey Optimizer campaigns.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "retrieve-inbox-state"
    }}>{`Retrieve Inbox State`}</h2>
    <p>{`To retrieve the inbox state, create a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MessagingInboxProvider`}</inlineCode>{` in your ViewModel and call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`getInboxUI()`}</inlineCode>{`. This returns a Flow of `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxUIState`}</inlineCode>{` objects that represent different states of the inbox:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`InboxUIState.Loading`}</inlineCode>{` - Content is being fetched`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`InboxUIState.Success`}</inlineCode>{` - Content was successfully loaded`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`InboxUIState.Error`}</inlineCode>{` - An error occurred while fetching content`}</li>
    </ul>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin-2"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`import androidx.lifecycle.ViewModel
import androidx.lifecycle.viewModelScope
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.aepcomposeui.state.InboxUIState
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.messaging.MessagingInboxProvider
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.messaging.Surface
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.messaging.InboxEventObserver
import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.SharingStarted
import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.StateFlow
import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.stateIn
import kotlinx.coroutines.launch

class InboxViewModel : ViewModel() {
    val inboxProvider = MessagingInboxProvider(Surface("inbox"))
    
    val observer = InboxEventObserver(inboxProvider)

    // Convert Flow to StateFlow for easier consumption in Compose
    val inboxUIState: StateFlow<InboxUIState> = inboxProvider.getInboxUI()
        .stateIn(
            scope = viewModelScope,
            started = SharingStarted.WhileSubscribed(5_000),
            initialValue = InboxUIState.Loading
        )

    // Function to manually refresh the inbox
    fun refresh() {
        viewModelScope.launch {
            inboxProvider.refresh()
        }
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Only content cards for which the user has qualified are returned. Client-side rules are defined in the Adobe Journey Optimizer campaign.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "display-inbox-in-compose-ui"
    }}>{`Display Inbox in Compose UI`}</h2>
    <p>{`The Inbox user interface is implemented using Jetpack Compose. To display the inbox, use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`AepInbox`}</inlineCode>{` composable with the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxUIState`}</inlineCode>{` from your ViewModel:`}</p>
    <InlineAlert variant="warning" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Do not embed AepInbox inside an unbounded container/lazy layout that scrolls in the same direction`}</p>
    <p><inlineCode parentName="p">{`AepInbox`}</inlineCode>{` uses a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`LazyColumn`}</inlineCode>{` for vertical layouts and a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`LazyRow`}</inlineCode>{` for horizontal layouts. The orientation is set when the inbox campaign is authored and published on Adobe Journey Optimizer UI. Embedding `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`AepInbox`}</inlineCode>{` inside an unbounded container/lazy layout that scrolls in the same direction — such as a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`LazyColumn`}</inlineCode>{` for a vertical scrolling inbox or a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`LazyRow`}</inlineCode>{` for a horizontal scrolling inbox — causes a runtime crash (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`IllegalStateException: Vertically/Horizontally scrollable component was measured with an infinity maximum constraints`}</inlineCode>{`).`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin-3"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
import androidx.compose.runtime.getValue
import androidx.lifecycle.compose.collectAsStateWithLifecycle
import androidx.lifecycle.viewmodel.compose.viewModel
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.aepcomposeui.components.AepInbox
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.aepcomposeui.style.InboxUIStyle

@Composable
fun InboxScreen(viewModel: InboxViewModel = viewModel()) {
    // Collect the inbox state from ViewModel
    val inboxUIState by viewModel.inboxUIState.collectAsStateWithLifecycle()

    // Display the inbox with default styling
    AepInbox(
        uiState = inboxUIState,
        inboxStyle = InboxUIStyle.Builder().build(),
        observer = viewModel.observer
    )
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "display-inbox-in-view-based-ui"
    }}>{`Display Inbox in View-based UI`}</h2>
    <p>{`To display an Inbox in a View-based (non-Compose) application, use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ComposeView`}</inlineCode>{` to embed the Compose UI within your Activity or Fragment:`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin-4"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`import android.os.Bundle
import androidx.activity.viewModels
import androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatActivity
import androidx.compose.ui.platform.ComposeView
import androidx.compose.ui.platform.ViewCompositionStrategy
import androidx.lifecycle.compose.collectAsStateWithLifecycle
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.aepcomposeui.components.AepInbox
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.aepcomposeui.style.InboxUIStyle

class InboxActivity : AppCompatActivity() {

    private val viewModel: InboxViewModel by viewModels()

    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)

        // Create ComposeView programmatically or inflate from XML
        val composeView = ComposeView(this).apply {
            setViewCompositionStrategy(ViewCompositionStrategy.DisposeOnViewTreeLifecycleDestroyed)
            setContent {
                val inboxUIState = viewModel.inboxUIState.collectAsStateWithLifecycle().value

                AepInbox(
                    uiState = inboxUIState,
                    inboxStyle = InboxUIStyle.Builder().build(),
                    observer = viewModel.observer
                )
            }
        }

        setContentView(composeView)
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "refreshing-inbox-data"
    }}>{`Refreshing Inbox Data`}</h2>
    <p>{`Inbox provides ways to refresh content cards:`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "programmatic-refresh"
    }}>{`Programmatic Refresh`}</h3>
    <p>{`You can programmatically refresh the Inbox using the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`refresh()`}</inlineCode>{` method on the provider:`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin-5"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`// In your ViewModel
fun refresh() {
    // Fetch latest content from server
    Messaging.updatePropositionsForSurfaces(listOf(surface))
    // Update the inbox UI with new content
    viewModelScope.launch {
        inboxProvider.refresh()
    }
}

// Call from your UI
Button(onClick = { viewModel.refresh() }) {
    Text("Refresh Inbox")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`This is useful for:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Refreshing on button taps`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Auto-refreshing at intervals`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Refreshing after specific app events`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "automatic-refresh-on-initial-load"
    }}>{`Automatic Refresh on Initial Load`}</h3>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`getInboxUI()`}</inlineCode>{` method automatically calls `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`refresh()`}</inlineCode>{` when first collected, so you don't need to manually trigger the initial load. The flow will emit:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`InboxUIState.Loading`}</inlineCode>{` - Immediately upon collection`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`InboxUIState.Success`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`InboxUIState.Error`}</inlineCode>{` - After the fetch completes`}</li>
    </ol>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "best-practices"
    }}>{`Best Practices`}</h2>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Pre-fetch Content`}</strong>{`: As described in `}<a parentName="p" {...{
            "href": "#fetch-inbox-settings-and-content-cards"
          }}>{`Fetch Inbox settings and Content Cards`}</a>{`, call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`updatePropositionsForSurfaces`}</inlineCode>{` early in your app lifecycle (e.g., Application class, splash screen, or after user login) to ensure content is cached and ready when the inbox is displayed.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Use ViewModel`}</strong>{`: Always manage the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MessagingInboxProvider`}</inlineCode>{` in a ViewModel to properly handle lifecycle and configuration changes. Use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`SharingStarted.WhileSubscribed`}</inlineCode>{` when converting the Flow to StateFlow to properly handle configuration changes:`}</p>
      </li>
    </ol>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin-6"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`val inboxUIState: StateFlow<InboxUIState> = inboxProvider.getInboxUI()
    .stateIn(
        scope = viewModelScope,
        started = SharingStarted.WhileSubscribed(5000),
        initialValue = InboxUIState.Loading
    )
`}</code></pre>
    <ol {...{
      "start": 3
    }}>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Wrap in Material Theme`}</strong>{`: When displaying the inbox, wrap it in your app's Material Theme to ensure proper styling of UI components:`}</li>
    </ol>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin-7"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`AppTheme {
    AepInbox(
        uiState = inboxUIState,
        inboxStyle = InboxUIStyle.Builder().build(),
        observer = viewModel.observer
    )
}
`}</code></pre>
    <ol {...{
      "start": 4
    }}>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Use Lifecycle-aware Collection`}</strong>{`: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`collectAsStateWithLifecycle()`}</inlineCode>{` instead of `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`collectAsState()`}</inlineCode>{` to automatically stop collecting when the UI is not visible, improving app performance and battery life:`}</li>
    </ol>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin-8"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`val inboxUIState by viewModel.inboxUIState.collectAsStateWithLifecycle()
`}</code></pre>
    <ol {...{
      "start": 5
    }}>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Surface Naming`}</strong>{`: Use descriptive surface paths that match your Adobe Journey Optimizer campaign configuration. Ensure the same surface string is used consistently between `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`updatePropositionsForSurfaces`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`MessagingInboxProvider`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</li>
    </ol>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin-9"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`// Use the same surface path in both places
val surface = Surface("inbox")
Messaging.updatePropositionsForSurfaces(listOf(surface))
val inboxProvider = MessagingInboxProvider(surface)
`}</code></pre>
    <ol {...{
      "start": 6
    }}>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Full Refresh Pattern`}</strong>{`: When implementing a manual refresh (e.g., pull-to-refresh or refresh button), call both `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`updatePropositionsForSurfaces`}</inlineCode>{` to fetch fresh content from the server and then `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`refresh()`}</inlineCode>{` to update the UI:`}</li>
    </ol>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />

      }}>{`fun onRefreshClicked() {
    // Fetch latest content from server
    Messaging.updatePropositionsForSurfaces(listOf(surface))
    // Update the inbox UI with new content

}
`}</code></pre>
    <ol {...{
      "start": 7
    }}>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Reuse Provider`}</strong>{`: Keep the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MessagingInboxProvider`}</inlineCode>{` instance alive as long as the Inbox view is visible. The provider maintains state and efficiently updates when content changes.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Handle Multiple Surfaces`}</strong>{`: If your app has multiple Inboxes (e.g., notifications, promotions), create separate providers with different surfaces:`}</p>
